summary.summary: Adds a native Swift port of the oref0 algorithm and plugs it into Trio as an opt‑in alternative to the existing JavaScript execution path. The port implements core algorithm modules in Swift (IoB, Meal/CoB, Autosens, DetermineBasal including Dynamic ISF), adds JSON bridging and conversion helpers, wiring in makeProfile to call both JS and Swift implementations for comparison, and includes tests and logging/replay support used to verify parity.
summary.userFacingImpact: Opt‑in feature: the app’s default remains the JavaScript oref path; users can enable “Use Swift Oref” in Settings → Algorithm → Additionals to run the Swift implementation instead (a green “Swift Oref” badge is shown when enabled). The PR includes shadow‑execution and unit test verification aimed at parity between JS and Swift; enabling the Swift path will cause the app to use Swift‑generated algorithm outputs instead of the JS outputs. The change was developed with parity checks (including a large replay of inputs) but because this is a replacement of dosing algorithm logic, a conservative human review / validation for live dosing is recommended before broad deployment.
